title: Can a MUD evaluate LLMs? A $99 proof of concept
excerpt: I'm the author of a paper my friends and I wrote after we were curious if a MUD, text games originating in the 1970s, could be used to evaluate LLMs. We've spent the last several months on nights and weekends running this experiment and writing the paper on just our personal computers with about $99 in API credits.
content_html:
Can a MUD evaluate LLMs? A $99 proof of concept (cruciblebench.ai)
104 points by Davisb135 22 hours ago | hide | past | favorite | 72 comments

I'm the author of a paper my friends and I wrote after we were curious if a MUD, text games originating in the 1970s, could be used to evaluate LLMs. We've spent the last several months on nights and weekends running this experiment and writing the paper on just our personal computers with about $99 in API credits.

Our experiment did have an interesting leaderboard but even more surprising was the measurements of each LLM. We scored each on four behavioral dimensions, two of which lean heavily on an LLM classifier. When we removed those two, one of the frontier models fell six positions. When we then checked the classifier against a second judge, the per-model agreement between them ranged from 85% to 22%. The aggregate kappa (0.04 on probe detection) indicated the instrument was noisy without saying which models the noise was hitting. The most affected model shared a model family with the classifier. This isn't proof of bias, just one observation we recorded.

We realize LLM judges can be unreliable, and while it wasn't our original intent to test this, it ended up being the most interesting finding. The divergence between the two judges is the finding we think generalizes to other judge-based benchmarks.

We emphasize this is just a proof of concept and not a validated benchmark. We prepared a thorough limitations section in the paper, including just 50 runs per model, overlapping CIs among the top models, no human raters, a tiny environment, etc.

Everything we did is publicly available, the paper and data are CC BY 4.0, while the code is MIT. The paper, transcripts, code, and complete API billing export can be found at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21386663

If you find issues, please let us know, that's why we're sharing this. We're currently designing Phase 2 and want it to be as robust as possible. We're looking at human baselines, multiple judges, more objectives, a larger environment, etc.

 help

Can a MUD evaluate LLMs? A $99 proof of concept (cruciblebench.ai)

I'm the author of a paper my friends and I wrote after we were curious if a MUD, text games originating in the 1970s, could be used to evaluate LLMs. We've spent the last several months on nights and weekends running this experiment and writing the paper on just our personal computers with about $99 in API credits.

sentences.en: Can a MUD evaluate LLMs?
sentences.zh: MUD 可以评估 LLM 吗？

sentences.en: A $99 proof of concept (cruciblebench.ai)104 points by Davisb135 22 hours ago | hide | past | favorite | 72 commentsI'm the author of a paper my friends and I wrote after we were curious if a MUD, text games originating in the 1970s, could be used to evaluate LLMs.
sentences.zh: 价值 99 美元的概念验证 (cruciblebench.ai)104 分，作者：Davisb135 22 小时前 |隐藏 |过去|最喜欢 | 72 条评论我是我和我的朋友们写的一篇论文的作者，我们好奇 MUD（起源于 20 世纪 70 年代的文字游戏）是否可以用来评估法学硕士。

sentences.en: We've spent the last several months on nights and weekends running this experiment and writing the paper on just our personal computers with about $99 in API credits.Our experiment did have an interesting leaderboard but even more surprising was the measurements of each LLM.
sentences.zh: 在过去的几个月里，我们在晚上和周末运行这个实验，并在我们的个人电脑上用大约 99 美元的 API 学分写论文。我们的实验确实有一个有趣的排行榜，但更令人惊讶的是每个法学硕士的测量结果。

sentences.en: We scored each on four behavioral dimensions, two of which lean heavily on an LLM classifier.
sentences.zh: 我们在四个行为维度上对每个维度进行评分，其中两个维度严重依赖于法学硕士分类器。

sentences.en: When we removed those two, one of the frontier models fell six positions.
sentences.zh: 当我们删除这两个模型时，其中一款前沿模型的排名下降了六位。

sentences.en: When we then checked the classifier against a second judge, the per-model agreement between them ranged from 85% to 22%.
sentences.zh: 然后，当我们与第二位法官检查分类器时，他们之间的每个模型一致性在 85% 到 22% 之间。

sentences.en: The aggregate kappa (0.04 on probe detection) indicated the instrument was noisy without saying which models the noise was hitting.
sentences.zh: 聚合 kappa（探头检测为 0.04）表明仪器有噪音，但没有说明噪音影响了哪些模型。

sentences.en: The most affected model shared a model family with the classifier.
sentences.zh: 受影响最大的模型与分类器共享一个模型系列。

sentences.en: This isn't proof of bias, just one observation we recorded.We realize LLM judges can be unreliable, and while it wasn't our original intent to test this, it ended up being the most interesting finding.
sentences.zh: 这并不是偏见的证明，只是我们记录的一个观察结果。我们意识到 LLM 法官可能不可靠，虽然这不是我们测试这一点的初衷，但它最终成为最有趣的发现。

sentences.en: The divergence between the two judges is the finding we think generalizes to other judge-based benchmarks.We emphasize this is just a proof of concept and not a validated benchmark.
sentences.zh: 两位法官之间的分歧是我们认为可以推广到其他基于法官的基准的发现。我们强调这只是一个概念证明，而不是经过验证的基准。

sentences.en: We prepared a thorough limitations section in the paper, including just 50 runs per model, overlapping CIs among the top models, no human raters, a tiny environment, etc.Everything we did is publicly available, the paper and data are CC BY 4.0, while the code is MIT.
sentences.zh: 我们在论文中准备了彻底的限制部分，包括每个模型仅运行 50 次、顶级模型之间重叠 CI、没有人类评估者、微小的环境等。我们所做的一切都是公开的，论文和数据是 CC BY 4.0，而代码是 MIT。

sentences.en: The paper, transcripts, code, and complete API billing export can be found at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21386663If you find issues, please let us know, that's why we're sharing this.
sentences.zh: 论文、成绩单、代码和完整的 API 计费导出可以在 https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21386663 找到。如果您发现问题，请告诉我们，这就是我们分享此内容的原因。

sentences.en: We're currently designing Phase 2 and want it to be as robust as possible.
sentences.zh: 我们目前正在设计第二阶段，并希望它尽可能强大。

sentences.en: We're looking at human baselines, multiple judges, more objectives, a larger environment, etc. help
sentences.zh: 我们正在考虑人类基线、多个法官、更多目标、更大的环境等。有帮助

blocks.html_en:
I'm the author of a paper my friends and I wrote after we were curious if a MUD, text games originating in the 1970s, could be used to evaluate LLMs. We've spent the last several months on nights and weekends running this experiment and writing the paper on just our personal computers with about $99 in API credits.

Our experiment did have an interesting leaderboard but even more surprising was the measurements of each LLM. We scored each on four behavioral dimensions, two of which lean heavily on an LLM classifier. When we removed those two, one of the frontier models fell six positions. When we then checked the classifier against a second judge, the per-model agreement between them ranged from 85% to 22%. The aggregate kappa (0.04 on probe detection) indicated the instrument was noisy without saying which models the noise was hitting. The most affected model shared a model family with the classifier. This isn't proof of bias, just one observation we recorded.

We realize LLM judges can be unreliable, and while it wasn't our original intent to test this, it ended up being the most interesting finding. The divergence between the two judges is the finding we think generalizes to other judge-based benchmarks.

We emphasize this is just a proof of concept and not a validated benchmark. We prepared a thorough limitations section in the paper, including just 50 runs per model, overlapping CIs among the top models, no human raters, a tiny environment, etc.

Everything we did is publicly available, the paper and data are CC BY 4.0, while the code is MIT. The paper, transcripts, code, and complete API billing export can be found at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21386663

If you find issues, please let us know, that's why we're sharing this. We're currently designing Phase 2 and want it to be as robust as possible. We're looking at human baselines, multiple judges, more objectives, a larger environment, etc.

content_html_zh: MUD 可以评估 LLM 吗？ 价值 99 美元的概念验证 (cruciblebench.ai)104 分，作者：Davisb135 22 小时前 |隐藏 |过去|最喜欢 | 72 条评论我是我和我的朋友们写的一篇论文的作者，我们好奇 MUD（起源于 20 世纪 70 年代的文字游戏）是否可以用来评估法学硕士。 在过去的几个月里，我们在晚上和周末运行这个实验，并在我们的个人电脑上用大约 99 美元的 API 学分写论文。我们的实验确实有一个有趣的排行榜，但更令人惊讶的是每个法学硕士的测量结果。 我们在四个行为维度上对每个维度进行评分，其中两个维度严重依赖于法学硕士分类器。 当我们删除这两个模型时，其中一款前沿模型的排名下降了六位。 然后，当我们与第二位法官检查分类器时，他们之间的每个模型一致性在 85% 到 22% 之间。 聚合 kappa（探头检测为 0.04）表明仪器有噪音，但没有说明噪音影响了哪些模型。 受影响最大的模型与分类器共享一个模型系列。 这并不是偏见的证明，只是我们记录的一个观察结果。我们意识到 LLM 法官可能不可靠，虽然这不是我们测试这一点的初衷，但它最终成为最有趣的发现。 两位法官之间的分歧是我们认为可以推广到其他基于法官的基准的发现。我们强调这只是一个概念证明，而不是经过验证的基准。 我们在论文中准备了彻底的限制部分，包括每个模型仅运行 50 次、顶级模型之间重叠 CI、没有人类评估者、微小的环境等。我们所做的一切都是公开的，论文和数据是 CC BY 4.0，而代码是 MIT。 论文、成绩单、代码和完整的 API 计费导出可以在 https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21386663 找到。如果您发现问题，请告诉我们，这就是我们分享此内容的原因。 我们目前正在设计第二阶段，并希望它尽可能强大。 我们正在考虑人类基线、多个法官、更多目标、更大的环境等。有帮助
